chief_complaint: A 51-year-old male patient presented with a history of COVID-19 infection and a history of untreated type 2 diabetes mellitus.
history: The patient was admitted to the hospital and received intravenous fluconazole for two weeks. He was discharged with oral fluconazole for three months and follow-up in an outpatient clinic. One month after hospital discharge, he presented with cardiorespiratory arrest.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG and blood tests were not mentioned, but the patient received intravenous fluconazole for two weeks.
